meta.title: BNYS Disease Reversal Protocols
meta.subtitle: Complete Clinical Reference — 3-Phase Protocol Family
meta.note: For use under qualified BNYS clinical supervision only. Do not modify or discontinue prescribed medications without physician oversight. All herbal supplements require clinician approval before initiation.

protocols.title: Hypertension (High Blood Pressure)
protocols.root_mechanism: Sympathetic nervous system overactivation + vascular endothelial inflammation + renin–angiotensin–aldosterone system (RAAS) dysregulation → sustained vasoconstriction and sodium retention → elevated systemic vascular resistance → elevated BP.

interventions.intervention: Slow deep breathing (4-7-8 technique) + sunlight (10 min)
interventions.effect: 4-7-8 breathing activates vagus nerve → reduces sympathetic outflow → lowers peripheral vascular resistance within minutes

interventions.intervention: Cold water spinal pack (cervical to lumbar)
interventions.effect: Cold stimulates posterior hypothalamus → paradoxically resets autonomic balance; reduces adrenal catecholamine surge

interventions.intervention: Abhyanga (warm sesame oil) head + foot massage
interventions.effect: Head massage stimulates baroreceptors in scalp vessels; foot massage activates reflexology zones linked to kidney/heart → lowers RAAS activity

interventions.intervention: Nadi Shodhana + Bhramari pranayama (humming bee breath)
interventions.effect: Bhramari produces nitric oxide in nasal sinuses → diffuses into pulmonary circulation → systemic vasodilation

interventions.intervention: Breakfast: DASH diet — oats, banana, flaxseed, low-fat milk
interventions.effect: Potassium (banana) competes with sodium at renal tubule → natriuresis; flaxseed omega-3 reduces vascular inflammation
Reduces splanchnic blood pooling; lowers cardiac preload; mild vagal activation

interventions.intervention: Beetroot juice (200 mL) or Arjuna bark decoction
interventions.effect: Dietary nitrates → nitric oxide via salivary bacteria → smooth muscle relaxation → direct vasodilation

interventions.intervention: Low-sodium meal: whole grains + leafy greens + legumes + curd
interventions.effect: Magnesium in greens relaxes vascular smooth muscle; calcium in curd modulates intracellular calcium balance

interventions.intervention: Yoga Nidra or 20-min rest in Savasana
interventions.effect: Induces theta brainwave state → cortisol drops 30–40% → reduces renal angiotensin release
Inversions improve baroreceptor sensitivity; forward bends reduce cardiac workload

interventions.intervention: Warm mustard oil foot bath + contrast arm bath
interventions.effect: Peripheral vasodilation shifts blood to periphery → reduces central pressure; mustard oil allyl isothiocyanate relaxes arterioles

interventions.intervention: Light dinner: soup + steamed veg + small grain portion; no salt after 5 PM
interventions.effect: Eliminating evening sodium prevents nocturnal BP surge; light meal reduces cardiac demand during sleep
Transdermal magnesium → smooth muscle relaxation; warmth lowers systemic vascular resistance

interventions.intervention: Progressive muscle relaxation or guided meditation
interventions.effect: Reduces muscle tension-mediated sympathetic feedforward; lowers overnight cortisol

relapse_rules.action: Return to Phase 2 for 2 weeks + daily spinal pack

yoga_sequence.effect: Nitric oxide production → vasodilation; vagal activation
Reduces cardiac sympathetic tone; stretches aorta
Baroreceptor reset; reduces venous return → lowers preload
Mild inversion; improves baroreceptor sensitivity
Opens chest; vagal activation via heart rate variability
Gentle abdominal compression; parasympathetic shift
Sustained nitric oxide; HRV improvement
Full parasympathetic dominance; cortisol normalisation

red_flags.action: Stop all stimulating interventions; urgent hospital referral immediately
Rule out hypertensive encephalopathy or stroke

red_flags.sign: Chest pain or shortness of breath
red_flags.action: Rule out hypertensive heart disease; urgent ECG

red_flags.sign: Sudden confusion, facial droop, arm weakness
red_flags.action: Stroke protocol — call emergency services
Hypotension — hold naturopathic vasodilating treatments; reduce drug dose

protocols.title: Obesity & Metabolic Syndrome
protocols.root_mechanism: Caloric excess + sedentary behaviour + circadian disruption → leptin resistance and ghrelin dysregulation → hyperphagia → visceral adipose tissue expansion → adipokine imbalance (↑TNF-α, IL-6, ↓adiponectin) → systemic insulin resistance and chronic low-grade inflammation.

interventions.effect: Cold activates brown adipose tissue (BAT) thermogenesis via norepinephrine → burns calories; morning exercise raises 24-h metabolic rate by 10–15%

interventions.intervention: Dry brushing full body (5 min) + Udvartana (herbal powder massage — triphala/mustard)
interventions.effect: Udvartana stimulates subcutaneous lymphatic drainage and lipolysis; friction increases skin temperature → local fat mobilisation
Kapalabhati raises metabolic rate 20% above resting; diaphragmatic contractions massage visceral organs → hepatic fat mobilisation

interventions.intervention: Intermittent fasting — eating window 12 PM–7 PM; break fast with protein + fat meal
interventions.effect: 18-h fast maximally depletes liver glycogen → sustained fat oxidation; later eating window aligns with peak thermogenic response
Clay draws heat from subcutaneous fat → increases local capillary blood flow → enhanced lipolysis in abdominal region

interventions.intervention: Green tea (1 cup) + apple cider vinegar (2 tbsp)
interventions.effect: EGCG in green tea inhibits COMT enzyme → prolongs norepinephrine action on fat cells; ACV reduces caloric intake by 200–275 kcal/day

interventions.intervention: Low-carb high-protein: 40% protein, 35% fat, 25% complex carb
interventions.effect: High protein: thermic effect 25–30% (vs 6–8% for carbs) → more calories burned in digestion; keeps satiety hormones (CCK, PYY) elevated

interventions.intervention: 10-min standing + calf raises + resistance band exercises
interventions.effect: Post-meal muscle contraction depletes muscle glycogen → prevents dietary glucose storage as fat; EPOC effect raises metabolism for 2 h

interventions.intervention: Full cold pack on abdomen + thighs
interventions.effect: Targeted cold exposure to adipose-rich areas → localised BAT activation and lipolysis via β3-adrenergic pathway
Resistance exercise builds lean muscle mass → raises resting metabolic rate by 50–100 kcal/day per kg muscle gained
Hyperthermia → heat shock proteins → improves mitochondrial efficiency; sweating eliminates toxins stored in fat cells

interventions.intervention: Last meal — protein + non-starchy vegetables only
interventions.effect: Closes eating window; evening meal with protein maintains muscle while restricting calories; no insulin spike before sleep
Magnesium improves insulin sensitivity; sulfate supports hepatic detoxification of fatty acid metabolites

relapse_rules.action: Return to Phase 2 + daily cold packs

yoga_sequence.effect: Full-body caloric burn (~13 cal/round); raises HGH
Core activation → visceral fat compression
Lateral stretch → stimulates liver and spleen
Opens thorax; increases lung capacity for fat oxidation
Compresses abdomen → massages pancreas and liver
Metabolic stimulation; fat-burning pranayama
Parasympathetic shift; growth hormone release for fat loss

avoid: All refined carbs (white rice, bread, maida)

red_flags.action: Rule out obesity hypoventilation syndrome; refer pulmonologist
Rule out cardiac event; stop exercise immediately
Rule out catabolism or malnutrition; check electrolytes
Check blood sugar; may need modified fasting protocol
Polysomnography required before intensifying exercise

protocols.title: Hypothyroidism
protocols.root_mechanism: Autoimmune (Hashimoto's) or iodine/selenium deficiency → impaired T4 synthesis and/or T4→T3 peripheral conversion → low cellular thyroid hormone → reduced basal metabolic rate, slowed gut motility, fluid retention, and mitochondrial dysfunction.

interventions.intervention: Cold shower (start 30 sec, build to 2 min) focusing cold water on neck/thyroid region
interventions.effect: Cold stimulates thyroid gland directly via increased TSH release from pituitary; norepinephrine activates thyroid hormone synthesis pathway

interventions.intervention: Neck/thyroid area Abhyanga with warm castor oil + gentle thyroid massage (circular)
interventions.effect: Castor oil ricinoleic acid reduces local thyroid inflammation (anti-Hashimoto's); manual massage increases thyroid blood flow → better iodine delivery
Ujjayi creates mild heat in thoracic region overlying thyroid; vibrational stimulation of thyroid via laryngeal contraction

interventions.intervention: Selenium-rich breakfast: Brazil nuts (2) + eggs + whole grain toast with coconut oil
interventions.effect: Selenium is essential cofactor for deiodinase enzymes (T4→T3 conversion); 2 Brazil nuts provide daily selenium requirement

interventions.intervention: Warm castor oil abdominal pack (liver area)
interventions.effect: Stimulates hepatic T4 to T3 conversion (70% of T3 is made in liver); castor oil enhances lymphatic detoxification of reverse T3

interventions.intervention: Ashwagandha decoction (500 mg in warm water)
interventions.effect: Withanolides in ashwagandha stimulate thyroid hormone synthesis; adaptogenic effect normalises HPA-HPT axis crosstalk

interventions.intervention: Iodine + selenium + zinc rich meal: seaweed, eggs, legumes, pumpkin seeds, wholegrains
interventions.effect: Iodine = thyroid hormone substrate; zinc required for TSH receptor function; avoid raw goitrogenic vegetables

interventions.intervention: 20-min brisk walk or light resistance exercise
interventions.effect: Exercise increases T3 receptor sensitivity in muscle; stimulates deiodinase activity; raises metabolic rate to compensate hypothyroid slowing

interventions.intervention: Warm sesame oil full-body massage (Sarvanga Abhyanga)
interventions.effect: Stimulates peripheral T3 receptor uptake; increases circulation to sluggish tissues; reduces myxoedematous fluid accumulation
Neck postures directly stimulate thyroid gland mechanically; inversions increase blood supply to thyroid

interventions.intervention: Contrast neck bath (alternating hot and cold compress on neck)
interventions.effect: Vascular pumping in thyroid region → flushes inflammatory cytokines; stimulates thyroid cell metabolism

interventions.intervention: Dinner: Easy-to-digest — moong dal soup, cooked vegetables, small amount grain
interventions.effect: Hypothyroid gut is sluggish — well-cooked, warm foods prevent constipation and bacterial overgrowth

interventions.intervention: Warm full-body bath with Himalayan pink salt (iodine source) + ginger powder
interventions.effect: Transdermal iodine absorption; ginger raises body temperature offsetting hypothyroid thermogenesis deficit

relapse_rules.action: Increase anti-inflammatory interventions; add turmeric protocol

yoga_sequence.effect: Direct pressure on thyroid gland → mechanical stimulation of hormone release
Arches neck; maximum stretch and blood supply to thyroid
Chin-lock stimulates thyroid; compresses anterior neck
Neck extension stimulates thyroid and parathyroid simultaneously
Jalandhara bandha — classic thyroid toning posture
Tongue protrusion + neck stretch stimulates thyroid + larynx
Laryngeal vibration stimulates thyroid mechanically; calms Hashimoto's autoimmune response
Parasympathetic dominance; reduces autoimmune Th1 cytokine activity

avoid: Raw cruciferous vegetables (broccoli, cabbage, cauliflower — goitrogenic when raw; cook thoroughly)

red_flags.action: Myxoedema coma risk — immediate hospital admission
Severe hypothyroid cardiac depression — urgent care
Immediate endocrinologist referral; foetal risk

red_flags.sign: Chest pain or pericardial effusion symptoms
red_flags.action: Hypothyroid heart disease — urgent echo
Rule out hypothyroid ileus

protocols.title: Polycystic Ovary Syndrome (PCOS)
protocols.root_mechanism: Insulin resistance → compensatory hyperinsulinaemia → ovarian theca cell stimulation → androgen excess (testosterone, DHEAS) → LH/FSH ratio imbalance → anovulation and follicular cyst formation. Hypothalamic GnRH pulse dysregulation perpetuates the cycle.

interventions.intervention: Sunlight exposure (10 min) + cold shower (2 min)
interventions.effect: Morning light resets SCN circadian clock → normalises nocturnal LH pulse amplitude; cold lowers elevated androgens via adrenal cortisol modulation

interventions.intervention: Castor oil pelvic Abhyanga (lower abdomen + sacrum area)
interventions.effect: Castor oil ricinoleic acid penetrates ovarian capsule → reduces cyst-associated inflammation; sacral massage stimulates parasympathetic pelvic nerves → improves ovarian blood flow
Balances hypothalamic-pituitary axis via autonomic modulation → normalises GnRH pulsatility → improves LH:FSH ratio

interventions.intervention: Low-GI high-protein: eggs + avocado + flaxseed smoothie
interventions.effect: Flaxseeds: lignans compete with oestrogen receptors → reduce estrogen dominance; low-GI breakfast lowers fasting insulin surge

interventions.intervention: Hot castor oil pack on lower abdomen (ovarian region)
interventions.effect: Heat + ricinoleic acid → increases local prostaglandin E1 → promotes follicular maturation; lymphatic stimulation reduces ovarian congestion
Spearmint reduces free testosterone by 30% (documented in RCTs); Shatavari (phytoestrogen adaptogen) normalises FSH and LH

interventions.intervention: Anti-androgen, anti-inflammatory: rainbow salad + legumes + seed cycling
interventions.effect: Seed cycling: pumpkin + flax seeds (follicular phase), sesame + sunflower (luteal) — provides zinc, selenium, omega-3 to balance hormones phase-specifically

interventions.intervention: Walk 20 min + pelvic floor exercises (Kegel 3 x 15)
interventions.effect: Exercise reduces androgen levels; pelvic floor strengthening improves uterine and ovarian circulation; GLUT4 translocation improves insulin sensitivity

interventions.intervention: Hip bath (warm, with neem or triphala decoction)
interventions.effect: Warm hip bath dilates pelvic vessels → reduces ovarian congestion and cyst pressure; neem is anti-androgenic
Hip-opening postures increase pelvic blood flow; twisting postures massage ovaries; pranayama regulates cortisol and androgens

interventions.intervention: Dinner: light, anti-inflammatory — cooked vegetables + dal + small portion grain
interventions.effect: Early light dinner reduces overnight insulin; evening insulin spikes directly stimulate androgen production

interventions.intervention: Warm Epsom salt bath with lavender essential oil
interventions.effect: Magnesium reduces stress-induced cortisol and DHEAS; lavender reduces anxiety-driven LH dysregulation via limbic system

exit_criteria.target: Reduction in cyst number or size

practices.dose_frequency: If cycle delays or pelvic pain returns

relapse_rules.action: Phase 2 for 6 weeks + pelvic packs daily

yoga_sequence.effect: Hip opening → increases pelvic blood flow; stretches ovarian ligaments
Passive hip opening; reduces ovarian congestion; stimulates parasympathetic pelvic nerves
Compresses pelvic floor → massages ovaries and uterus mechanically
Improves venous drainage from pelvis; reduces ovarian cystic pressure
Massages ovaries + adrenals; reduces androgen excess from adrenal source
Hypothalamic-pituitary axis regulation via ANS balancing
Cortisol reduction; HPA-HPO axis resetting

avoid: soy in excess (phytoestrogen disruption at high doses)

red_flags.action: Rule out premature ovarian failure or secondary amenorrhoea
Rule out ovarian torsion — surgical emergency
Rule out ovarian/adrenal tumour; urgent endocrinology
Collaborate with reproductive endocrinologist before protocol intensification
PCOS-associated T2DM — add diabetes protocol

protocols.title: Rheumatoid Arthritis (RA)
protocols.root_mechanism: Molecular mimicry or gut dysbiosis → loss of immune tolerance → Th1/Th17 cell activation → synovial macrophage infiltration → TNF-α, IL-1β, IL-6 excess → synovitis, pannus formation, cartilage erosion, and systemic inflammation.

interventions.intervention: Warm paraffin bath or warm water soak for hands/feet (15 min)
interventions.effect: Heat reduces synovial viscosity; promotes synovial fluid circulation; reduces morning stiffness by dilating periarticular capillaries

interventions.intervention: Gentle joint mobilisation (range-of-motion exercises — all joints)
interventions.effect: Passive-to-active ROM preserves cartilage nutrition (avascular cartilage feeds via synovial fluid diffusion during movement)
Reduces systemic cortisol → lowers IL-6 and CRP; vagal activation reduces pro-inflammatory macrophage polarisation

interventions.intervention: Anti-inflammatory: fatty fish / flaxseed + turmeric milk + berries
interventions.effect: Omega-3 EPA/DHA competitively inhibit arachidonic acid → reduce leukotriene B4 and PGE2 production; turmeric curcumin blocks NF-kB

interventions.intervention: Cold pack on acutely inflamed joints (hot pack for stiff joints — clinician choice)
interventions.effect: Cold on inflamed joints: reduces prostaglandin synthesis; slows nerve conduction → analgesia. Hot on stiff: reduces adhesions, increases extensibility
Boswellic acid inhibits 5-lipoxygenase (5-LOX) → blocks leukotriene B4 (primary neutrophil chemoattractant in RA); ginger's gingerols inhibit COX-2

interventions.intervention: Anti-inflammatory Mediterranean-type: olive oil, vegetables, whole grains, legumes, no red meat
interventions.effect: Mediterranean diet reduces CRP 20%; olive oil oleocanthal = natural ibuprofen equivalent; avoids arachidonic acid from red meat

interventions.intervention: Gentle 20-min pool walk or chair yoga
interventions.effect: Aquatic exercise offloads joints (90% body weight supported); maintains muscle strength without synovial trauma

interventions.intervention: Castor oil joint pack on affected joints + medicated Mahanarayan oil massage
interventions.effect: Castor oil ricinoleic acid: potent anti-inflammatory (reduces PGE2); Mahanarayan oil: sesame + 50 herb blend penetrates periarticular tissue
Gentle yoga reduces TNF-α by 18% (documented); improves joint proprioception; HPA axis regulation
Magnesium sulfate reduces synovial prostaglandins; transdermal absorption; sulfate supports cartilage GAG synthesis

interventions.intervention: Light anti-inflammatory dinner: dal khichdi + turmeric + ghee
interventions.effect: Ghee butyrate reduces intestinal permeability (leaky gut drives RA autoimmunity); turmeric synergises with fat for absorption

interventions.intervention: Medicated herbal oil massage (Dhanwantharam oil) on all joints
interventions.effect: Traditional Ayurvedic joint-penetrating oil blend; sesame oil base transports anti-inflammatory alkaloids across skin-joint barrier

practices.dose_frequency: Lifelong — no red meat, no refined sugar

relapse_rules.action: Return to Phase 2 + daily castor packs

yoga_sequence.effect: Maintains shoulder ROM; gentle synovial fluid distribution
Systematic joint mobilisation from toes to neck; classic RA therapy
Reduces lower limb oedema; anti-gravity joint decompression
Reduces TNF-α via cortisol regulation; pain modulation via DMN
Spinal facet mobilisation; reduces systemic inflammatory cytokines
Most potent anti-inflammatory pranayama — reduces IL-6 directly

avoid: nightshade vegetables (tomato, eggplant, pepper — trial-avoid in flares)

red_flags.action: Rule out septic arthritis — joint aspiration required urgently
Rule out RA-associated interstitial lung disease
Rule out scleritis or uveitis — urgent ophthalmology
Rule out atlanto-axial subluxation in cervical RA
Biologics consultation required

protocols.title: Osteoarthritis (OA) — Knee & Hip
protocols.root_mechanism: Mechanical overload + chondrocyte senescence → proteoglycan loss (collagen type II degradation) → subchondral bone sclerosis → osteophyte formation → synovial inflammation (secondary) → cartilage thinning and joint space narrowing.

interventions.intervention: Hot paraffin wax bath on knee/hip or warm mustard oil joint pack
interventions.effect: Heat increases synovial fluid viscosity and distribution; reduces subchondral bone pain by dilating periarticular vasculature; relaxes periarticular muscle spasm

interventions.intervention: Alternating hot-cold pack on joint (2 min each x 5 cycles)
interventions.effect: Contrast therapy reduces secondary synovial oedema; vascular pumping flushes inflammatory metabolites from joint space

interventions.intervention: Gentle pranayama: Anulom Vilom + Bhramari (10 min)
interventions.effect: Reduces systemic IL-6 and TNF-α; lowers pain catastrophising via limbic modulation → reduces central sensitisation

interventions.intervention: Collagen-rich anti-inflammatory: bone broth / egg white + vitamin C foods + turmeric
interventions.effect: Vitamin C is essential for collagen type II synthesis in chondrocytes; turmeric curcumin inhibits MMP-3/13 (cartilage-degrading enzymes)

interventions.intervention: Mahanarayan oil deep massage around (not on) joint + drainage technique
interventions.effect: Periarticular massage reduces muscle spasm and compensatory joint loading; oil penetrates bursa → reduces periarticular inflammation
Boswellic acid reduces cartilage-degrading leukotriene B4; guggulsterones reduce COX-2 expression in chondrocytes

interventions.intervention: Anti-inflammatory joint-supportive: selenium, zinc, omega-3 rich foods; avoid nightshades
interventions.effect: Selenium reduces oxidative damage to chondrocytes; zinc is cofactor for SOD — chondrocyte antioxidant protection

interventions.intervention: Pool exercise or chair exercise (non-weight-bearing strengthening)
interventions.effect: Quadriceps strengthening reduces knee joint loading by 4 kg per 1 kg muscle gained; water buoyancy prevents shear forces on damaged cartilage

interventions.intervention: Cold pack on swollen joint (if warm/inflamed) or hot pack (if stiff, not inflamed)
interventions.effect: Precise application: acute oedema → cold (reduces inflammatory exudate); chronic stiffness → heat (increases collagen extensibility)
Non-impact joint strengthening; periarticular muscle building; gentle ROM maintenance without cartilage compression

interventions.intervention: Epsom salt soak (full body or local knee/foot bath)
interventions.effect: Magnesium sulfate reduces periarticular muscle tension; sulfate is substrate for chondroitin sulfate synthesis in cartilage

interventions.intervention: Dinner: bone broth soup + anti-inflammatory vegetables + small protein
interventions.effect: Evening bone broth provides glycine, proline, hydroxyproline — direct cartilage repair substrates during overnight tissue regeneration

practices.dose_frequency: If joint swells or pain > 5 VAS

relapse_rules.action: Phase 1 for 14 days; assess for aspiration

yoga_sequence.effect: Strengthens quads without joint compression; improves posture
Decompresses knee joint; reduces intra-articular pressure
Glute + hamstring strengthening; reduces knee valgus stress
Gentle hamstring stretch; reduces posterior knee tension
Hip abductor strengthening without loading
Calf strengthening; improves ankle-knee chain mechanics
Pain modulation via endorphin release; reduces central sensitisation
Reduces knee oedema; pain-free recovery position

eat: vitamin C foods (amla, bell pepper, kiwi)

red_flags.sign: Sudden joint locking or giving way
red_flags.action: Rule out meniscal tear or loose body — orthopaedic review

red_flags.sign: Hot, red, swollen joint with fever
red_flags.action: Rule out septic arthritis — emergency aspiration
Surgical evaluation for joint replacement

red_flags.sign: Rapidly progressive joint space loss on X-ray
red_flags.action: Aggressive rheumatology referral

red_flags.sign: Numbness or weakness in limb below joint
red_flags.action: Rule out nerve entrapment or vascular compromise

protocols.title: Bronchial Asthma
protocols.root_mechanism: Atopic sensitisation + environmental triggers → mast cell degranulation → Th2-mediated eosinophilic bronchial inflammation → bronchial smooth muscle hyperreactivity + mucus hypersecretion + subepithelial fibrosis → airflow obstruction.

interventions.intervention: Nasal irrigation (Jala Neti with saline) + warm steam inhalation with eucalyptus
interventions.effect: Nasal irrigation clears allergens and reduces nasal eosinophil load; eucalyptus 1,8-cineole is mucolytic and bronchodilatory

interventions.intervention: Buteyko breathing exercise (nasal-only, reduced volume breathing)
interventions.effect: Buteyko method raises CO2 → smooth muscle relaxation via Bohr effect → bronchodilation; breaks hyperventilation-bronchospasm cycle

interventions.intervention: Pranayama: Anulom Vilom + slow Kapalabhati (30 strokes/min only)
interventions.effect: Slow Kapalabhati increases alveolar ventilation without triggering bronchospasm; Anulom Vilom reduces mast cell reactivity via vagal modulation

interventions.intervention: Anti-inflammatory, anti-allergic: turmeric milk, vitamin C fruits, honey + ginger
interventions.effect: Quercetin (onion, apple) stabilises mast cells; vitamin C reduces histamine levels; ginger inhibits 5-LOX and prostaglandin E2

interventions.intervention: Chest mustard oil pack (warm) + percussion massage on upper back
interventions.effect: Mustard oil allyl isothiocyanate increases bronchial blood flow; percussion vibrates bronchial walls → loosens mucus plugs → aids expectoration
Vasaka leaf alkaloid vasicine is potent bronchodilator and mucolytic (10x more than bromhexine); licorice glycyrrhizin inhibits 11β-HSD → anti-inflammatory

interventions.intervention: Anti-histamine, anti-inflammatory: no dairy, no processed food, rainbow vegetables + fish
interventions.effect: Dairy increases mucus viscosity; processed food contains sulphites (asthma trigger); omega-3 shifts LTB4 to LTB5 → weaker bronchospasm mediator

interventions.intervention: Slow 20-min nasal-only walk in pollution-free environment
interventions.effect: Nasal breathing warms, humidifies and filters air → reduces exercise-induced bronchospasm; aerobic exercise improves FEV1 long-term

interventions.intervention: Cold water chest pack (apply on upper chest/sternum)
interventions.effect: Cold → reflex vasodilation on rewarming → improved bronchial mucosal circulation; reduces eosinophilic infiltration in bronchial wall

interventions.intervention: Yoga sequence (see below) — NO Kapalabhati in active wheeze
interventions.effect: Chest-opening postures increase FRC; diaphragm strengthening reduces accessory muscle use; inversions drain sinus congestion

interventions.intervention: Steam inhalation with ajwain (carom seeds) + turmeric
interventions.effect: Ajwain thymol is potent bronchodilator; steam hydrates bronchial mucosa; reduces nocturnal mucus plugging

interventions.intervention: Light easy-to-digest dinner: no dairy, no cold foods
interventions.effect: Nocturnal asthma worsens with large meals (diaphragm compression); avoid dairy's mucus-increasing effect before sleep

interventions.intervention: Warm mustard oil chest and back massage
interventions.effect: Overnight mucolysis; reduces nocturnal bronchospasm via parasympathetic skin stimulation; warmth prevents cold-triggered bronchoconstriction

interventions.frequency: Daily (15 min — no Kapalabhati in wheeze)

practices.dose_frequency: If wheeze returns or on polluted days

relapse_rules.action: Phase 2 for 4 weeks + daily chest packs

yoga_sequence.effect: Trains diaphragm; increases tidal volume; reduces FRC deficiency
Opens chest; stretches intercostal muscles; improves thoracic compliance
Maximum chest expansion; stretches anterior chest wall; drains posterior lung zones
Stretches bronchial tree via neck-chest expansion; stimulates thymus (immune modulation)
Diaphragm strengthening; thoracic spine extension improving rib cage mobility
Postural drainage of upper lobes; reverses gravitational mucus pooling
Reduces bronchial hyperreactivity; improves peak flow by 12% in 4 weeks (studied)
Passive chest opening; reduces residual volume

avoid: sulphite-containing foods (wine, dried fruits, packaged foods)

red_flags.action: Acute severe asthma — administer rescue bronchodilator; hospital emergency immediately
Life-threatening attack — call emergency services

red_flags.sign: Rescue inhaler > 3 x in 24 h with no relief
red_flags.action: Status asthmaticus risk — urgent hospitalisation
Rule out infective exacerbation/pneumonia
Rule out foreign body or tumour — urgent imaging

protocols.title: Non-Alcoholic Fatty Liver Disease (NAFLD)
protocols.root_mechanism: Insulin resistance → hepatic de novo lipogenesis ↑ + impaired VLDL export → hepatic triglyceride accumulation → mitochondrial oxidative stress → inflammatory cytokine production (TNF-α, IL-6) → hepatocyte injury → progression to NASH.

interventions.effect: Lemon limonene stimulates hepatic phase I detoxification enzymes (CYP450); morning light activates CLOCK genes controlling hepatic metabolism

interventions.intervention: Dry brushing + castor oil liver pack (right upper abdomen)
interventions.effect: Castor oil pack increases hepatic lymphatic flow; ricinoleic acid reduces hepatic stellate cell activation
Kapalabhati mechanically massages liver via diaphragmatic contractions; increases portal venous return → flushes fatty hepatocytes

interventions.intervention: Intermittent fast until 11 AM; break fast with protein + vegetable meal
interventions.effect: Fasting activates hepatic autophagy (lipophagy) — liver literally self-digests stored triglycerides via autophagosomes

interventions.intervention: Warm castor oil pack on liver (right hypochondrium)
interventions.effect: Documented to increase liver lymph flow 3x; activates hepatic NK cells (innate immunity); reduces hepatic inflammation and fibrosis markers
Silymarin inhibits lipid peroxidation in hepatocytes; upregulates glutathione synthesis; dandelion root increases hepatic bile flow → cholesterol excretion

interventions.intervention: Liver-supportive: cruciferous vegetables, beets, garlic, olive oil, NO fructose
interventions.effect: Cruciferous indole-3-carbinol upregulates hepatic phase II detox; beet betaine prevents hepatic fat accumulation; fructose directly drives DNL
Exercise reduces hepatic fat independently of diet (increases FGF21 → hepatic fat oxidation); abdominal breathing massages portal vasculature

interventions.intervention: Coffee enema or clay abdominal pack (under BNYS supervision)
interventions.effect: Coffee enema increases glutathione transferase 600–700%; clay pack draws hepatic heat → increases local lipolysis in fatty liver
Liver twist postures flush portal blood through hepatic sinusoids; inversions reverse hepatic venous pooling
Sulfate is rate-limiting substrate for hepatic sulfation (phase II detox); magnesium cofactor for over 20 hepatic enzymes

interventions.intervention: Early, light dinner — dal + cooked vegetables; NO alcohol, NO fructose
interventions.effect: Liver's VLDL export peaks during day; evening meals with fat/fructose overwhelm export capacity → nocturnal fat accumulation
Curcumin upregulates PPAR-alpha (hepatic fat-burning transcription factor); piperine increases curcumin bioavailability 2000%

relapse_rules.action: Phase 2 for 6 weeks + daily castor packs
Full Phase 1 restart + addiction counselling referral

yoga_sequence.effect: Maximum liver compression and release → portal blood flushing
Abdominal pressure on liver; stimulates hepatic blood flow
Bilateral liver massage; stimulates bile duct drainage
Compresses liver against spine; stimulates portal flow
Reverses hepatic venous congestion; improves sinusoidal flow
Direct hepatic mechanical massage via rapid diaphragm contractions
Allows maximal hepatic perfusion at rest; growth hormone release supports hepatic repair

avoid: Fructose (fruit juices, HFCS, sweetened beverages — #1 driver of hepatic DNL)

red_flags.action: Rule out NASH cirrhosis or biliary obstruction — urgent liver panel
Decompensated cirrhosis — immediate hospital admission
Variceal bleed — emergency surgical intervention
Hepatic encephalopathy — ICU level care

red_flags.sign: ALT > 3x upper limit on any test
red_flags.action: Pause all supplements; urgent hepatology referral

protocols.title: Irritable Bowel Syndrome & Chronic Constipation
protocols.root_mechanism: Gut dysbiosis + increased intestinal permeability (leaky gut) + ENS hypersensitivity + microbiome imbalance → enteroendocrine dysfunction (serotonin dysregulation — 95% of serotonin is gut-derived) → abnormal motility (IBS-C or IBS-D) + visceral hypersensitivity + low-grade mucosal inflammation.

interventions.intervention: 2 glasses warm water (40°C) + abdominal self-massage (clockwise, 5 min)
interventions.effect: Warm water stimulates gastrocolic reflex within 30 min; clockwise massage follows colonic anatomy → mechanically propels stool; stimulates 5-HT4 receptors in gut wall

interventions.intervention: Triphala decoction (1 tsp in warm water, taken on empty stomach)
interventions.effect: Triphala's sennosides stimulate myenteric plexus; chebulinic acid in haritaki is cholinergic → increases colonic smooth muscle contraction

interventions.intervention: Squat position on toilet or footstool (Squatty Potty technique)
interventions.effect: Squatting straightens the anorectal angle from 90° to 135° → reduces straining force needed by 65%; relaxes puborectalis muscle
Diaphragmatic breathing massages transverse colon via up-down diaphragm excursion; vagal stimulation enhances migrating motor complex (MMC) initiation

interventions.intervention: High-fibre prebiotic breakfast: oats + psyllium husk (1 tsp) + banana + curd (probiotic)
interventions.effect: Psyllium husk forms gel → increases stool bulk and moisture; banana resistant starch feeds Bifidobacterium; curd Lactobacillus colonises gut

interventions.intervention: Warm castor oil pack on abdomen (especially in constipation)
interventions.effect: Castor oil ricinoleic acid binds EP3 receptors in small intestine → increases fluid secretion and smooth muscle contraction; topical pack absorbs through abdominal wall
Ginger 6-gingerol accelerates gastric emptying and small bowel transit; fennel anethole is antispasmodic for IBS-D, prokinetic for IBS-C

interventions.intervention: Gut-repair meal: cooked easily digestible, high-fibre, probiotic, low-FODMAP
interventions.effect: Low-FODMAP reduces fermentable substrate for dysbiotic bacteria; cooked vegetables easier on sensitised ENS; probiotic with meal survives gastric acid better
Walking increases MMC frequency; physical movement activates intrinsic enteric neurons directly
Clay minerals (smectite) adsorb toxins and excess fermentation gases; soothing temperature reduces visceral hypersensitivity

protocols.title: Anxiety & Depression (Neuroinflammatory Model)
protocols.root_mechanism: HPA axis dysregulation + gut-brain axis disruption + mitochondrial dysfunction → neuroinflammation (elevated IL-6, TNF-α crossing BBB) → BDNF reduction → hippocampal neurogenesis impairment → serotonin / dopamine depletion → mood dysregulation, anxiety hyperarousal, and anhedonia.

interventions.effect: Morning sunlight resets cortisol awakening response; suppresses nocturnal melatonin; activates serotonin synthesis in raphe nuclei; cold activates norepinephrine + BDNF release

interventions.intervention: Abhyanga (warm sesame oil full body — especially scalp and feet)
interventions.effect: Scalp Abhyanga reduces cortisol and substance P; increases oxytocin (touch hormone); sesame tyrosine is dopamine precursor; foot massage stimulates hypothalamic parasympathetic centres
4-7-8 activates parasympathetic via extended exhalation; Bhramari generates nitric oxide + vagal activation → reduces amygdala hyperactivity by 20% (fMRI documented)

interventions.intervention: Neurotrophin-supportive: eggs + blueberries + walnuts + turmeric oats
interventions.effect: Eggs: choline → acetylcholine; blueberries anthocyanins cross BBB → reduce neuroinflammation; walnuts DHA → neuronal membrane fluidity; oats tryptophan → serotonin precursor
Grounding reduces cortisol by normalising diurnal pattern; reduces inflammatory cytokines (electron transfer from earth); sunlight D3 synthesis → reduces neuroinflammation
Cold on forehead and occiput → reduces cortisol output from HPA; anti-inflammatory effect on glial cells; vagal nerve at neck stimulated
Ashwagandha reduces cortisol 27% in RCTs; withanolides increase GABA-A receptor sensitivity; Brahmi bacosides stimulate BDNF production + improve hippocampal neurogenesis

interventions.intervention: Gut-brain axis support: probiotic curd + prebiotic fibre + omega-3 + tryptophan-rich
interventions.effect: 95% serotonin made in gut → gut health directly determines mood; Lactobacillus produces GABA; omega-3 reduces neuroinflammation; tryptophan from protein → serotonin pathway
Walking increases BDNF (equal to antidepressants in mild-moderate depression — multiple RCTs); gratitude activates ventromedial PFC → downregulates amygdala

interventions.intervention: Warm sesame oil Shirodhara (if available) OR self-forehead oil press
interventions.effect: Shirodhara (continuous warm oil on forehead) induces theta brainwaves; reduces cortisol by 35%; documented reduction in GAD-7 score by 6 points in 2 weeks
Yoga Nidra reduces cortisol 40%; increases dopamine 65% (PET scan documented); stimulates GABA production; reduces amygdala volume in 8 weeks

interventions.intervention: Cold/contrast foot bath + warm chest compress
interventions.effect: Foot bath activates serotonin-containing enterochromaffin cells in skin; chest warmth reduces anxiety-associated chest tightness; stimulates cardiac vagal tone

interventions.effect: Social connection is #1 protective factor for depression; evening tryptophan → overnight serotonin and melatonin synthesis

interventions.intervention: Warm full-body Epsom salt bath + lavender essential oil
interventions.effect: Magnesium is NMDA receptor antagonist → reduces glutamate-driven anxiety; sulfate supports cortisol clearance; lavender linalool binds GABA-A receptors

interventions.intervention: Yoga Nidra or body scan meditation (30 min) + no screens after 9 PM
interventions.effect: Screens suppress melatonin via blue light; Yoga Nidra induces NREM-like sleep state → hippocampal memory consolidation; theta waves promote neuroplasticity

relapse_rules.action: IMMEDIATE psychiatric referral; do not manage in BNYS alone
